item.title: Kids do feel the cold. So why won't they wear a jumper?
item.description: Do kids really not feel the cold like adults do? Or are they just expressing their independence? And when should you insist?

item.title: Why supporters of India's 'Cockroach' movement marched on parliament
item.description: Thousands of protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party have marched on India's parliament and clashed with the authorities. Here's why.

item.title: Former Bishop of Broome Christopher Saunders faces first day of sex abuse trial
item.description: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

item.title: Federal Labor MP Luke Gosling 'categorically denies' incident with NT administrator
item.description: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash
item.description: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

item.title: Collingwood coach Craig McRae says Bobby Hill will 'always be welcome' at club as he gets set to cut contract
item.description: With Collingwood set to be at full strength for its Adelaide clash, coach Craig McRae sends a message to missing star Bobby Hill, who is set to quit the club.

item.title: Indonesia sentences leader of baby-trafficking ring to seven years' jail
item.description: Lie Siu Luan, 70, was the leader of a group of 19 people accused of trafficking babies in Indonesia and Singapore.

item.title: Hanson's European travel benefactor still a mystery, but One Nation leader says it was not taxpayers
item.description: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Inquest to determine whether Adelaide man killed his mother and staged her death
item.description: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

item.title: China's EV Revolution
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say as they call for licensing deals at Andrew Olle lecture
item.description: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

item.title: Killed UK politician Ann Widdecombe hit 21 times with hammer, prosecutors say
item.description: Joshua Kerry, 28, appears in the Westminster Magistrates' Court charged with murder over a killing that has shocked the public and political establishment.

item.title: Trump says US will target suspected Iranian nuclear site 'pretty soon'
item.description: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 22: Join us for a trip around the world
item.description: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

item.title: Penny Lane is Dead pairs gory horror with a queer love story
item.description: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

item.title: Jewish schools say kids hurting as security getting in way of education
item.description: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

item.title: Albanese survived three near-fatal car accidents. His career is just as unlikely
item.description: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

item.title: Artificial intelligence company Firmus plans to use state-owned agricultural irrigation water
item.description: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

item.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error
item.description: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

item.title: JackJumpers Roth's advice for inaugural Tasmania Devils AFL coach Ken Hinkley
item.description: Being out amongst the people will be key for incoming Tasmania Devils coach Ken Hinkley, according to the man who led the state to NBL glory in 2024.

item.title: BlueScope Steel hit with multiple workplace safety breaches after contractor's death
item.description: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

item.title: Outback reunions cancelled by rain damage as tourism suffers for second year
item.description: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

item.title: Rebel Wilson awaits ruling in defamation battle with fellow actor
item.description: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

item.title: Players and fans made this World Cup a success, but the politics shouldn't be forgotten
item.description: The biggest ever World Cup brought diverse communities together and galvanised its non-traditional footballing co-hosts, but the concerns raised from the start have not disappeared in the positive afterglow.

item.title: Elusive fogbow photographed at Cobar, western NSW
item.description: Cold weather and moist conditions combine to produce a fogbow in an unlikely place.

item.title: Bradley died months after national antipsychotic medicine shortages, coronial inquest hears
item.description: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

item.title: Cameron McEvoy returns to Glasgow for Commonwealth Games after almost giving up on swimming
item.description: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

item.title: CLP government passes amended child protection laws in 'significant overhaul' for Northern Territory
item.description: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

item.title: Boy, 16, charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister at Bentley home
item.description: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

item.title: $30m biochar facility proposed in central Queensland, with Rio Tinto a major buyer
item.description: Rio Tinto has signed an agreement with an Australian company for a biochar product that could help replace coal used in its emissions-intensive Queensland refineries.

item.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino accused of 'destroying the football industry' with World Cup expansion
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino comes in for more heavy criticism, this time at the hands of La Liga boss Javier Tebas.

item.title: Berkeley Vale mother Louise Farley took up soccer for her dying son
item.description: After Spencer Farley was diagnosed with terminal cancer, he asked his mum to start playing soccer. In the 12 months since his death, the game has kept her going.

item.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff prompts discovery and disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains
item.description: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

item.title: Rescued beluga whales transferred from Canada to US aquariums.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Godzilla star Kaylee Hottle killed in Maryland car crash
item.description: US investigators say speed was a likely factor in the crash that killed actor Kaylee Hottle, 18.

item.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE brain disease diagnosis
item.description: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

item.title: These grieving mothers want you to say their babies' names
item.description: Women who have lost children talk about continuing in the wake of such grief, and how people stepped up, or stepped away.

item.title: As The Odyssey tops the box office is Christopher Nolan the new king of Hollywood?
item.description: The Odyssey's director Sir Christopher Nolan has a stellar catalogue. Interstellar, even. But as his new film smashes box office records is it safe to crown him the new king of Hollywood?

item.title: Bike thefts at Darwin Men's Shed have disrupted deliveries to remote NT communities
item.description: About 60 donated bicycles have been stolen from the Darwin Men's Shed storage site, delaying the organisation's plans to support remote communities.

item.title: Australia's 'Doctor Jihad' Tareq Kamleh reportedly found alive in Iraqi jail
item.description: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

item.title: Dairy giant Lactalis to close Longwarry factory in Gippsland by 2027
item.description: Lactalis has announced it will close its dairy factory in Longwarry, in Gippsland, with a phased transition of production to other sites.

item.title: Beluga whales flown from closed Canadian marine park to new homes in the US
item.description: Six beluga whales from a defunct marine park in Canada have been successfully moved to new homes in the US.

item.title: The claims and evidence behind popular anti-aging and wellness treatments
item.description: Wellness and anti-aging clinics are popping up across Australia. But do their treatments work and are they safe?

item.title: Jury in former Broome bishop sex trial warned not to be swayed by Catholic church prejudice
item.description: The lawyer for a former Catholic bishop who allegedly sexually abused three young men tells his trial the incidents did not happen, and warns the jury not to be affected by possible prejudice against the church.

item.title: Pod of dolphins dies within 24 hours after stranding at Albany's Princess Royal Harbour
item.description: Twelve pantropical spotted dolphins have died after a pod was first found stranded on an Albany beach on Tuesday afternoon.

item.title: Shane Brendan Wesley, 42, charged with attempted murder over Kalgoorlie stabbing
item.description: Police allege the 42-year-old stabbed a woman multiple times with a knife while dealing drugs on Kalgoorlie's main street.

item.title: Network 10 shifts 10 News+ from weekdays after one year on air
item.description: Network 10 says its 10 News+ news and current affairs program is moving from its nightly weekday slot and will be aired on Sunday nights only.

item.title: Tasmanian health department tries to block probe after referring hospital death to coroner
item.description: Tasmania's health department is challenging a coronial review into allegations that a clinical director falsified death certificates at Launceston General Hospital.

item.title: Pauline Hanson labels domestic violence 'two-way street', urging women 'not to put up with it'
item.description: The One Nation leader says women should not "put up with" domestic violence, as she highlights the cost-of-living pressures on families.

item.title: Woman in Melbourne businessman rape case told 'lie after lie', defence says
item.description: A woman who accused a well-connected Melbourne businessman of rape is a serial liar and made "false allegations" in court, defence lawyers say.

item.title: Charge against Amy Tossoun over alleged Mount Buller drink spiking thrown out
item.description: A Victorian magistrate has struck out a drink spiking charge against Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun.

item.title: Proposed $40b, 185ha data centre to use six times Top End's annual electricity consumption
item.description: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

item.title: Inquest into Annette Mason's 1989 death hears woman took it into her 'own hands' to solve murder
item.description: A long-running inquest into the death of Annette Mason in 1989 has heard from a woman who took it into her "own hands" to solve the 15-year-old's murder.

item.title: Eels defend Jarome Luai's 2027 NRL signing ahead of Chiefs move
item.description: Parramatta coach Jason Ryles is unapologetic about agreeing to Jarome Luai's one-season stopover at the Eels.

item.title: Australian multi-millionaire arrested by Secret Service, pardoned by Trump and sued by Apple before coming home
item.description: Chris Wade was arrested by the Secret Service in Dallas in the early 2000s. It took him on a journey from FBI hacker to Mar-a-Lago and eventually the White House before he came back to Australia to buy his favourite pub.

item.title: Young Filly rape trial shown photographs of alleged victim's injuries
item.description: A doctor testifies at a sexual assault trial about the injuries allegedly inflicted by an internationally renowned YouTube star on an infatuated fan in his Perth hotel room after a concert.

item.title: Judge lifts non-publication order on Alan Jones's hearings
item.description: Details of allegations against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones can be revealed, after a non-publication order was lifted by a judge.

item.title: Labor to back Fair AI Taskforce to give workers a say over artificial intelligence
item.description: Labor will respond to growing anxiety about the rise of artificial intelligence with a new taskforce to give Australian workers a greater say in how the technology shapes the nation.

item.title: French modelling scout with links to Epstein found dead, prosecutors say
item.description: A French modelling scout with close ties to the late US financier Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors say.

item.title: Nina Kennedy named Australian flag-bearer for Glasgow Commonwealth Games opening ceremony
item.description: Reigning Commonwealth Games gold medallists Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge will carry the Australian flag and King's baton at the Glasgow 2026 opening ceremony.

item.title: Australia's property market enters downturn as prices fall for first time in three years
item.description: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years, bringing an end to the recent housing boom.

item.title: Trump threatens Iranian power plants and bridges if ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US strikes have already widened from the south to western and central areas of Iran in recent days.

item.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach 'historic' nuclear power deal in Gulf kingdom
item.description: Israeli officials and US politicians from both parties have voiced concern that a civilian nuclear project in Saudi Arabia could be used to eventually develop nuclear weapons.

item.title: Court date set for trial of captured Venezuelan president Maduro
item.description: US commandos captured Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores in January this year and took them to New York, where they now face a number of charges including narcoterrorism.

item.title: Logies quiz: Get caught up before Australian TV's night of nights
item.description: As the Logies approach, on August 16, we thought we would test your knowledge of the beloved but daggy Australian television awards and this year's nominees.

item.title: Putin's nefarious rise skewered in satirical drama The Wizard of the Kremlin
item.description: Cannes darling Oliver Assayas tackles the shadowy forces of our time in his latest film, The Wizard of the Kremlin.

item.title: Irish citizenship applications soar as Australians chase passport power
item.description: Australians are racing to secure Irish citizenship, with new figures showing applications have more than doubled in the past three years as people seek the right to live, work and study across the EU.

item.title: Ex-ASIO source with information on Bondi gunmen denied visa to give evidence at royal commission
item.description: The man who previously warned ASIO about the Bondi gunmen will not be able to give sensitive and classified evidence at the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

item.title: How One Nation is winning over regional Australia
item.description: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?

item.title: Tongan women taken to China for marriage triggers police investigation
item.description: The ABC has spoken to multiple Tongan women who have been lured into an arranged marriage syndicate operating in China, with allegations of passports being confiscated and tens of thousands of dollars changing hands.

item.title: How the 2026 Commonwealth Games ended up in Glasgow and what they will look like
item.description: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

item.title: Labor conference kicks off with gas, gambling and Gaza
item.description: Anthony Albanese may have been a keen participant in robust conference debates in years gone by but is now using his authority to enforce a much more controlled process.

item.title: Data centre company Firmus urged to be 'good corporate citizen' after water plan accidentally goes public
item.description: A Tasmanian farmer has urged a data centre builder to "be a good corporate citizen" after it accidentally revealed a plan to tap into a water source earmarked for agriculture for cooling purposes at one of its facilities.

item.title: Inside CPAC London: Charlatans, conspiracy theories and loud snoring
item.description: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

item.title: Inside Queensland’s trap houses where vulnerable children are allegedly drugged and sexually abused
item.description: The ABC has spoken with experts, advocates and residential care workers as part of a 19-month investigation into the sexual exploitation of children in state care.

item.title: Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games daily schedule in Australian time
item.description: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

item.title: Victorian opposition promises $850 million to build new West Gippsland hospital if elected
item.description: The Victorian Coalition pledges $850 million to build a new West Gippsland hospital if it wins the November election.

item.title: AFL's first publicly gay player Leigh Ryswyk calls for respect in Crows-Pies match
item.description: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

item.title: Narendra Modi facing the biggest youth protest movement of his rule, the Cockroach Janta Party
item.description: A student who started a youth uprising across India is fast becoming the biggest challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 12-year reign.

item.title: Should you yell at dogs? Experts weigh in after Barnaby Joyce's pooch spray
item.description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce is back in the spotlight after some harsh words to his dog during a live TV interview, but experts are divided about what works when it comes to pulling a canine into line.

item.title: Parks Victoria plan proposes full closure of about 100 Arapiles climbing routes
item.description: A proposed draft amendment of a park management plan, first written in 1991, would close about 100 climbing routes, while more than 3,000 others would remain open.

item.title: When Gordon Bray and his Homebush Boys High schoolmates ruled their rugby world
item.description: It might be more than 60 years ago, but Homebush Boys High's 1965 Waratah Shield win remains a fond memory for the likes of Gordon Bray, John Symond and John Coates.

item.title: Nhulunbuy's Captain Cook Shopping Centre renamed after sale to Gumatj Aboriginal Corporation
item.description: British explorer James Cook never visited Nhulunbuy on his voyages, but his namesake shopping complex stood there for decades — until now.

item.title: Charlotte MacInnes plans to appeal her failed Rebel Wilson defamation case. Here's how
item.description: As lawyers for actor Charlotte MacInnes plan to appeal a failed defamation case outcome against Hollywood star Rebel Wilson, a solicitor says the judge's interpretations could be grounds for a retrial or a different verdict.

item.title: Private investors invited to build tourism developments in four NT national parks
item.description: The Northern Territory government says opening up national parks to private investors will unlock their "tourism potential", but an expert fears the move could mean less access for locals and budget-conscious visitors.

item.title: North West Queensland eyes $100m farming expansion in move from cattle to crops
item.description: A $5.3 million investment aims to expand cropping across North West Queensland, with ambitious targets for cotton, grain and pulses in traditionally cattle country.

item.title: Weightlifter Kiana Elliott puts 'best foot forward' for Glasgow Commonwealth Games
item.description: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

item.title: OpenAI model hacks startup after going rogue during testing
item.description: OpenAI has announced one of its AI models broke containment during testing before hacking into another AI startup.

item.title: T-rex skeleton sells for $70 million at auction
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare allegedly forces training cancellation after protesting his transfer
item.description: Star Wolverhampton striker Tolu Arokodare allegedly refuses to leave the training pitch in protest over his transfer to a Turkish club.

item.title: Pilbara artist Jenna Pickering transforms metal and wood scraps into prized portraits
item.description: Pilbara artist Jenna Pickering transforms found bush objects into hyper-realistic portraits as artist-in-residence at the remote Cossack Art Awards.

item.title: Daily charter flights to Beef Australia an option amid Rockhampton accommodation shortage
item.description: Beef Australia is exploring new ways to get visitors to travel to Rockhampton, amid a scarcity of hotel rooms and some accommodation advertised for $24,000 for the week of the event in May 2027.

item.title: WA historical journals were key to revealing this man's family story
item.description: For most of his life, Cam Sojan knew nothing of his family history until he stumbled upon a historical society in Western Australia's Midwest last year, which revealed his Sikh ancestry.

item.title: Owner of illegal Diamond Creek dump fined a record $60k
item.description: The owner of one of Victoria's worst illegal dump sites has been handed a record fine of $60,000 and ordered to pay an expected $1 million to clean up the polluted property in Melbourne's outer north-east.

item.title: Indigenous-owned town hopes statue will highlight world-class fishing spots
item.description: On the far west coast of SA, this Aboriginal community requires a permit to enter, meaning people usually drive on by. But new public art at the roadhouse, bought by the community, aims to boost tourism and financial independence.

item.title: Men's netball growing in popularity but sport faces barriers to growth
item.description: More young men are taking up netball at a competitive level. Competition organisers say there is less stigma around the sport among boys, with plenty of talent emerging.

item.title: Nina Kennedy ticks off another goal at Glasgow Commonwealth Games as she nears the end
item.description: Australia's Commonwealth Games flag-bearer is trying to stop and smell the roses after a brutal year that threatened to expedite the end of her career.

item.title: Mount Gambier family takes racing club to court over alleged rubbish dump
item.description: A family discovered a large waste dump, allegedly created by a South Australian horse racing club, as they began foundation work on their home, court documents allege.

item.title: Adelaide man charged with producing and possessing AI-generated child exploitation material
item.description: SA Police have arrested and charged a man from Adelaide's northern suburbs for the production and possession of AI-generated child exploitation material.

item.title: Australia's newest media outlet used to be the One Nation Supporters Network
item.description: A "right of centre" website claiming to be news, with more than 42,000 Facebook followers, may not be as independent as it says. An ABC NEWS Verify investigation has found the site uses AI and a fake news editor.

item.title: Want to read more? Comics, audiobooks and social media can help
item.description: More than half of us want to read more. Here's how to find your gateway genre and make that happen.

item.title: Labor crosses off conference bingo card, Liberals brandish blue brochure
item.description: Australia's two major parties are deliberating their futures this week with Labor's national conference and the Liberal Party's glossy new pamphlet.

item.title: Woodside Energy fails in court bid to force activists to hand over stink-bomb plans
item.description: Energy giant Woodside fails in its Supreme Court bid to force climate activists to hand over documents about the planning of a stink-bomb protest which prompted the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

item.title: Nate Caddy to play for Bombers against Hawks after heart scare
item.description: Essendon interim coach Dean Solomon says Nate Caddy will play in Saturday's match against Hawthorn following his recovery from a heart scare.

item.title: Sydney social housing experiencing extreme heat due to lack of standards, study says
item.description: A new study led by Western Sydney University researchers has found a lack of thermal safety standards in social housing is leading to tenants being exposed to unsafe extreme heat events.

item.title: Proposed $200m blue gum timber hub to boost Victorian jobs and building materials
item.description: The timber manufacturing hub would transform blue gum wood chips into timber beams for construction.

item.title: Orca rams into sunfish so hard it explodes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi says she sleeps three hours or less per night
item.description: It's unlikely Sanae Takaichi intended to start a national conversation when she tweeted about how much rest she gets. A sleep expert says her schedule would be exacting a heavy toll.

item.title: Net Zero Commission says NSW unlikely to meet emissions reduction targets
item.description: The NSW Net Zero Commission recommends tighter planning controls for the approval of coal extensions and data centres, as it predicts the state will fall short of its legislated 2030 emissions reduction target.

item.title: Why experts are worried an OpenAI model decided to hack Hugging Face
item.description: An experimental AI broke out of its containment and hacked a company. Why has this got experts worried?

item.title: Ex-officer apologises during inquest into fatal shooting of Sydney man Jesse Deacon
item.description: A former sergeant has apologised to the mother of a Sydney man who was shot dead by police while experiencing a mental health episode.

item.title: Football Victoria forces Preston Lions to play behind closed doors over fan conduct
item.description: The Preston Lions, associated with the Macedonian Australian community, will be forced to play without spectators after some of its fans displayed offensive banners at a game last weekend.

item.title: Man fined for wearing swastika T-shirt outside antisemitism royal commission
item.description: A man who wore a T-shirt with a banned Nazi symbol outside the antisemitism royal commission in Sydney has been convicted and fined $1,500.

item.title: Jonovia Bridal shop worker Dominique Rolland fronts court on $121,000 fraud charge
item.description: The 53-year-old wanted to plead guilty to a fraud charge but a magistrate has ruled the case must be referred to a higher court with greater sentencing powers.

item.title: Origin Energy confirms unauthorised access and disclosure of customer data
item.description: Energy company Origin confirms there has been unauthorised access to and disclosure of some customers' data, a day after it announced it was investigating a "potential" breach.

item.title: Through my eyes: Jess Gallagher
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police investigate AI-generated explicit images linked to Gold Coast teacher
item.description: Police are investigating alleged AI-generated explicit material found on a Gold Coast teacher’s account.

item.title: Former MP Fraser Ellis convicted of extra counts of deception for misusing Country Members Allowance
item.description: Former SA state MP Fraser Ellis now stands convicted of seven counts of deception after failing to have his original convictions thrown out on appeal, and having several acquittals overturned.

item.title: Tennant Creek and Devils Marbles Hotel prepare for total eclipse chasers in 2028
item.description: About 10,000 eclipse chasers are expected to descend on the Northern Territory's Barkly region in two years to stand in the shadow of the moon.

item.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at Flinders Medical Centre
item.description: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

item.title: Driver Jordan Kenyon boasted of drink driving, weeks before killing 12-year-old girl
item.description: County Court judge Michael Tinney says Jordan Kenyon's decision to get behind the wheel after consuming the drug GHB was like playing "Russian roulette" with the general public.

item.title: Jail for man who shot and killed Paul Davy during argument over dog
item.description: The family of a man who was fatally shot through a screen door during an argument over a dog say the sentence handed to his killer is "bittersweet".

item.title: ACT Chief Minister Andrew Barr speaks about his deputy's resignation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Court hears complainant's evidence questioned in trial of former NSW Police officer accused of rape
item.description: The defence team of a former NSW Police officer accused of raping a 17-year-old girl in 1998 has told the court that the complainant's claims are unreliable.

item.title: Perth man Mihael Vrhovsek jailed for attempted murder of woman in Rivervale attack
item.description: A man who "doggedly" tried to kill a woman in a prolonged attack in suburban Perth caught on CCTV has is been jailed for 16 years.

item.title: Perth's road to nowhere deadlock resolved after four-year-delay over Aboriginal heritage concerns
item.description: One of Western Australia's longest bridges will be built to fix a road to nowhere in Perth's east — resolving a four-year long deadlock over fears the bridge would destroy an ancient rock shelter and billabong.

item.title: Taiwanese woman sentenced to 11 years in prison for attempted murder of Australian exchange student
item.description: Taiwanese media has reported Yang Jinping has been sentenced for the attempted murder of Brisbane exchange student Alex Shorey in 2023.

item.title: 'Fascinating world': McRae questions booing directed at Quaynor
item.description: Collingwood franks its strong run of form with an impressive 34-point win over Adelaide, as Crows fans defy pleas from the club and boo Isaac Quaynor.

item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Penrith flyer breaks record in comeback Panthers win
item.description: Thomas Jenkins has claimed a slice of history in Penrith's unconvincing win over Parramatta in the western Sydney derby.

item.title: The dilemma in the Big Bash League privatisation push
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Glasgow Commonwealth Games starts with bowls win for Australia's Kelsey Cottrell and Dawn Hayman
item.description: Australia's first victory of the Commonwealth Games comes in a surprise extra session as the bowlers start before the opening ceremony.

item.title: Chinese diplomats leave Labor conference in Adelaide after requesting Taiwan representatives be ejected
item.description: The incident could stir fresh tensions between China and Australia in the wake of several recent high-profile disputes.

item.title: Death toll climbs to 82 after flash flooding in India, Afghanistan and Pakistan
item.description: At least 82 people have died and more than 650,000 have been impacted after heavy rain and flash flooding in India, Pakistan and Afghanistan.

item.title: Player agents express concern as Super Netball pay talks roll into Commonwealth Games
item.description: Messy and drawn-out pay negotiations are once again overshadowing a major tournament for the Aussie Diamonds.

item.title: AUKUS shipyards in Adelaide to receive $4.6 billion funding lift
item.description: The billions of dollars in additional funding is another step towards making Australia's nuclear submarine program "a reality".

item.title: Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Donald Trump
item.description: Donald Trump is insisting the US deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear energy industry has one major condition — the Arab kingdom must formally recognise the State of Israel.

item.title: Petrol supply could 'tighten' as oil jumps to more than $US100 a barrel
item.description: The entry of the Houthi rebels into the war between the US and Iran has threatened another vital shipping route through the Middle East.

item.title: From political satire to puerile stunts, here's what's in cinemas this month
item.description: Emmy Award-winning Adolescence co-creator Stephen Graham tackles teen delinquency in a very different way in The Good Boy.

item.title: ABC news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog
item.description: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

item.title: King Charles opens Glasgow 2026 during ceremony buoyed by Scottish pride
item.description: King Charles III officially opens the Commonwealth Games as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators.

item.title: Charli xcx follows Brat with her most reflective album yet: Music, Fashion, Film
item.description: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

item.title: How China won the car wars with cheap EVs loaded with technology
item.description: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

item.title: Gippsland fire brigade pushes for compensation after 22 members diagnosed with cancer
item.description: In Nar Nar Goon's fire brigade, 22 volunteers have been diagnosed with cancer. But as members work to obtain their work insurance cover, almost half of the members are not covered by the state's presumptive legislation.

item.title: Small businesses shop around for more credit as economic pressures mount
item.description: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

item.title: Origin data breach could fuel wave of AI-powered scams, cyber experts warn
item.description: The theft of names, addresses and phone numbers may seem less serious than stolen bank details, but cyber experts say AI is changing what criminals can do with that information.

item.title: The texts that warned Gladys Berejiklian about a looming Liberal corruption scandal
item.description: Nineteen months before making the parliamentary speech that sparked a corruption inquiry into NSW Liberal figures and their relationship with a fugitive property developer, parliamentary secretary Ray Williams sent an urgent text message to his boss, premier Gladys Berejiklian.

item.title: Big Build corruption allegations warrant Victorian inquiry, expert says
item.description: One of Australia's leading public policy academics says a "Queensland-style" commission of inquiry is needed into Victoria's Big Build.

item.title: Conspiracy theories about AusAlert test spreading online
item.description: From wrapping your phone in foil to secret phones, ABC NEWS Verify takes a look into some of these false claims and real concerns about AusAlert to try to answer a few questions.

item.title: Probe launched into claims platypus burrow destroyed by Tasmanian council flood prevention works
item.description: An investigation is launched after wildlife advocates say a platypus burrow was destroyed by an excavator being used for flood mitigation works in northern Tasmania.

item.title: Child safety advocates warn more needs to be done to help teens leaving state care from homelessness
item.description: Queensland Family and Children's Commissioner Luke Twyford says the situation is "profoundly upsetting".

item.title: Maxwell Douglas's Ipswich childhood was marked by climate disaster. His response went global
item.description: Equal parts captivating and unsettling, young people from Ipswich to Japan are taking to the streets to dance "flood" style.

item.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

item.title: US tracks Chinese icebreakers that visit Australian ports
item.description: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

item.title: Regional funding a 'sleeper issue' that could end in rare fight on Labor conference floor
item.description: The ALP's 50th National Conference kicked off with the rise of One Nation, local council budgets and regional voter frustrations leading negotiations.

item.title: Country footy's female participation has surged since AFLW, but long-standing facilities are not built to fit them
item.description: Macedon Football Netball Club is proud to do things its way. But it is in a pinch with its women and girls team.

item.title: Calls for more elder abuse support services as reports climb in Queensland
item.description: Reports of elder abuse in Queensland have steadily increased in recent years, with women being the most common victims. Advocates say urgent investment is needed for prevention and response.

item.title: Indonesia's baby traffickers target poor families as demand grows
item.description: Police in Indonesia are more regularly busting baby trafficking and illegal adoption rings, with officials worried poverty may be driving the practice.

item.title: New mobile coverage maps still not accurate, customers say
item.description: Telcos were forced to publish mobile phone coverage maps to new standards last month, but some Telstra customers say they still do not reflect reality.

item.title: Qld police arrest man over AI-generated posters targeting multiple women
item.description: The 52-year-old is accused of posting fake sexualised images of women, including their full names, in public places across south-east Queensland over five years.

item.title: With Yvette Berry resigning from cabinet, ACT Labor faces a monumental reckoning
item.description: A government that's been in power for almost 25 years is unlikely to have an unblemished record. But while ACT Labor ministers have weathered controversy on many occasions, Yvette Berry was different.

item.title: Sydney house prices falling in 'premium locations', but rising in historically affordable outer suburbs
item.description: First home buyers are "less likely to be affected" by Sydney's housing price and unit downturn as historically affordable areas have increased, a property expert says.

item.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy scores 'own goal' by firing defence minister at critical time in war
item.description: The Ukrainian president's decision to fire his popular, tech-savvy 35-year-old Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov last week baffled many. Analysts say what happens next could be pivotal to the war.

item.title: Fears Luddenham overlooked as opening of Western Sydney International Airport looms
item.description: Luddenham is a five-minute drive from Western Sydney International Airport (WSI), but some residents say they are missing out on the benefits it should be bringing them.

item.title: Live: Angus Taylor dismisses Trump's 'forced labour' tariff justification
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says he's "dead against" the Trump administration's tariffs on Australian goods set to come into force later today, dismissing the notion it has anything to do with modern slavery laws. Follow live.

item.title: Family says NT school failed in its duty of care after alleged indecent assault of disabled 12-year-old child
item.description: A Northern Territory family has accused the Department of Education of failing in its duty of care for their 12-year-old girl, after she was allegedly indecently assaulted at a school that caters for children with disabilities.

item.title: Divers using cameras and computer models to bring Mount Gambier underwater caves to the public
item.description: Cave divers have swapped measuring tapes and pencils for 3D modelling to map a patchwork of underwater caves below houses and streets in Mount Gambier.

item.title: Live: RBA rate hike calls grow as oil surges
item.description: Markets and some commentators are now expecting an August RBA interest rate hike following a surge in oil prices above $US100 a barrel and persistent inflation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the live markets blog.

item.title: Fremantle has one hand on the AFL minor premiership, but West Coast is eyeing a Western Derby upset
item.description: Fremantle sits atop the mountain, with one hand firmly grasping the AFL's minor premiership. West Coast is finally showing sustained improvement. Both sides have something to gain; one has a lot to lose.

item.title: Trump administration confirms new 12.5 per cent tariff for Australia
item.description: The levy will be placed on exports from later today, superseding a 10 per cent tariff that was set to expire. Dozens of other countries will also be hit, but Australia's tariff rate is among the highest.

item.title: Australian Stingers look to replicate Sydney 2000 in USA rematch, men's Sharks stun champions Spain
item.description: As a four-year-old, Stingers skipper Bronte Halligan attended the Sydney Olympic Games, where the Aussies secured a famous victory against the USA, and 26 years later, they aim to do it all again in a World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Tour de France 18th stage winner Richard Carapaz survives close call with spectator
item.description: Richard Carapaz almost hits a fan distracted by a TV camera as the Ecuadorian claims the 18th stage of the Tour de France.

item.title: Worker dead, another injured as truck rolls over vehicle at Queensland mine
item.description: One man is dead and another has been seriously injured after their vehicle was struck by a giant mining truck at a Central Queensland coal site.

item.title: Counterfeit notes seized as North Queensland grapples with 'waves' of fake cash
item.description: A woman in her 40s has been arrested and charged with fraud and possessing counterfeit notes after allegedly using the money to buy a second-hand motorbike from a teenager in North Queensland.

item.title: How an insect repellent left almost a dozen hospital staff sick
item.description: How did an insect repellent get to the hospital? Who released it? And how did almost a dozen staff at the hospital get sick?

item.title: First suspected positive case of H5 bird flu detected in Queensland
item.description: It's the first wild, migratory seabird in Queensland to return a suspect positive case for H5 bird flu.

item.title: Shania Twain on the inspiration behind her latest album
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Glasgow Commonwealth Games opening ceremony turns adversity into delight
item.description: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

item.title: Patient has hen's party, wedding and learns to walk again during hospital stay
item.description: When Christine Olsen-Lane suffered a life-threatening fall, she could not have foreseen the unexpected joy the next 11 months would bring.

item.title: Budjerah's debut album Gentleman has been a long time coming
item.description: Two-time ARIA Award-winning music artist Budjerah has just released his long-awaited debut album and announced an official tour.

item.title: Paramedic reconnects with patient after 11,000th helicopter rescue in WA
item.description: Hiker Lucy Fairweather reunites with her "heroes" as the RAC Rescue helicopter celebrates its 11,000th rescue in WA.

item.title: Waratahs brimming with confidence ahead of Super Rugby Women's grand final against Drua
item.description: Chasing a seventh Super Rugby Women's title, the Waratahs are in a confident mood ahead of Saturday's season decider against the Drua in Fiji.

item.title: NSW abalone industry faces coastline closure due to severe stock decline
item.description: As the NSW government considers closing 350 kilometres of shoreline to abalone harvesting, divers say the severe decline of the shellfish is due to mismanagement.

item.title: Gaps in Australian law leave thousands of stateless people in 'limbo'
item.description: As a Rohingya person from Myanmar, Asma was denied an official nationality. Migration experts say statelessness, an issue faced by over 8,000 people in Australia, remains poorly understood.

item.title: White House will not fight Tate brothers extradition to UK
item.description: US President Donald Trump will not stop the extradition of influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan to Britain on sex crime charges, the White House has said.

item.title: MotoGP to rev up motorcycle culture and pilgrimages to City of Churches
item.description: The arrival of the Tour Down Under in 1999 inspired a significant uptick in Lycra-wearing cyclists in and around Adelaide. Will the MotoGP have a similar effect for high-performance motorcycles?

item.title: Norway set to file complaint over Trump's push to overturn red card
item.description: Norway's soccer federation says football is on a "slippery slope" after FIFA overturned a World Cup red card under pressure from US president Donald Trump.

item.title: Man linked to Isla Bell death fails to have case thrown out
item.description: Marat Ganiev was seeking a permanent stay on his case, with his lawyer arguing the multiple changes to the prosecution case were an abuse of process.

item.title: Four Corners withdraws episodes by sacked reporter due to accuracy concerns
item.description: The ABC has withdrawn the Four Corners programs Meth Highway and Cocaine Nation by sacked reporter Mahmood Fazal due to concerns about accuracy.

item.title: TGA issues warning over GLP-1-based drugs potential to cause rare eye disorder
item.description: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has issued a safety warning for a rare, but serious risk of vision impairment from taking GLP-1-based drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy.

item.title: Matt Wright Federal Court case over Chris 'Willow' Wilson's death could take another year before making it to trial
item.description: A Federal Court case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright could take another year before making it to trial. The lawsuit has been brought by Danielle Wilson, the widow of Mr Wright's TV co-star Chris "Willow" Wilson, who died in a 2022 chopper crash.

item.title: Tony Modra's medical records allegedly inappropriately accessed by SA Health staff
item.description: Three SA Health staff are being investigated for alleged inappropriate access to the private medical records of AFL star Tony Modra, while a further five are being "assessed" to determine whether their access to the records was appropriate.

item.title: Sam Kerr says A-League must find a way to bring Matildas back, as she thrives in return to US
item.description: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is already thriving in the next chapter of her career with Gotham FC in the NWSL, but she wants the A-League Women to get back to its previous glory days, with more investment in the semi-professional league.

item.title: Police launch fresh appeal to identify victims of alleged childcare abuser Hamish Tait
item.description: Police are still trying to identify a child from a Putney childcare centre in Sydney's north who was found pictured on electronic devices seized from Hamish Tait as part of the investigation.

item.title: Kyle Chalmers praises Gina Rinehart over bonuses amid warning to not thank billionaire
item.description: Swimming star Kyle Chalmers praises billionaire Gina Rinehart over bonuses for Commonwealth Games medals, as a report suggests athletes have been told not to thank the mining magnate.

item.title: Microsoft says Teams outage across Asia-Pacific now 'mitigated'
item.description: The company says the issue stopped users "joining or creating Microsoft Teams meetings or calls for users in the Asia-Pacific region".

item.title: Major review flags concerns over Australia's ill-defined national security offences
item.description: A major review warns parts of Australia's national security laws could undermine democracy and press freedom.

item.title: Police offer $500,000 reward to help solve Dianne Angwin's 1997 cold case murder
item.description: Police allege 50-year-old Dianne Angwin was fatally stabbed in her Bowen unit in 1997.

item.title: Petrol prices tipped to rise across Australia, squeezing household budgets
item.description: Australians are being urged by a peak motorist association not to panic buy fuel amid a flare-up of tensions in the Middle East, saying prices are nowhere near April's record high.

item.title: Trump has slapped a 12.5 tariff on Australian goods. What does that mean?
item.description: The tariffs are supposed to combat forced labour, but one expert says the US president was just looking for another legal way to tax foreign imports.
